ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 represents chocolate milk; the 'less sugar' variant is a formulation sub-type not separately coded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate milk (less sugar) as a distinct food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 ml (one glass), providing about 128 kcal, 6.5 g protein, and 18 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate Milk (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chocolate Milk (Less Sugar), record ID F01030002. All core macros and secondary nutrients are from this source with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 1.0 g/100 g from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 01103 (Milk, chocolate, fluid, commercial, lowfat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.

gi_source.title: Chocolate milk, GI value 40 — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous to reduced-sugar flavoured milk
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 40 is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate milk (low-fat, reduced sugar). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 18.0) / 100 = 7.2 for a 200 g serving. The 'less sugar' variant is treated as analogous to reduced-sugar chocolate milk in the Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage classification; sugar 5.99 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold for beverages
hypertension.guidance: Despite low sodium (59 mg/100 g), this beverage qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with 5.99 g sugar/100 g; SSB consumption independently raises blood pressure risk (AHA 2018), so individuals with hypertension should choose plain unsweetened low-fat milk instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.99 g sugar/100 g and near-zero dietary fibre (0.23 g/100 g); liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: This 'less sugar' chocolate milk still contains 5.99 g sugar/100 g with minimal fibre buffering, classifying it as an SSB that is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; plain unsweetened milk or a no-added-sugar chocolate milk alternative is strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: SSB sugar content (5.99 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (approximately 1.0 g/100 g); regular SSB intake is associated with elevated triglycerides and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and sodium profile of this beverage is favourable for cardiovascular health, but the residual sugar content (5.99 g/100 g) means regular consumption as an SSB may contribute to hypertriglyceridaemia; limit intake and prefer plain low-fat milk for daily dairy needs.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (5.99 g/100 g) with near-zero fibre; calcium content (148 mg/100 g) is beneficial for bone protection in RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: While the calcium content (148 mg/100 g) supports bone health relevant to RA, the added sugar (5.99 g/100 g) with minimal fibre does not meet the anti-inflammatory dietary criterion; plain unsweetened milk provides the same calcium benefit without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate Milk (Less Sugar) is a processed dairy beverage made from low-fat cow's milk flavoured with cocoa powder or chocolate syrup, formulated to contain reduced sugar compared to standard chocolate milk. Per 100 g it provides 64 kcal, 3.25 g protein, 1.56 g fat, 9.0 g carbohydrate, and 5.99 g sugar, with 148 mg calcium and 59 mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 40 (low), but the sugar content of 5.99 g/100 g still classifies this beverage as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and international dietary guidelines. Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension due to the SSB classification. The calcium content makes it a useful bone-health beverage for the general population and for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when consumed in moderation. It is commonly consumed as a snack or post-exercise recovery drink in Singapore and globally. The low-fat formulation reduces saturated fat intake compared to full-fat chocolate milk, making it marginally better for cardiovascular health, though plain unsweetened milk remains the preferred dairy choice for individuals managing cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: Is chocolate milk (less sugar) good for diabetics?
Can I drink reduced sugar chocolate milk if I have hypertension?
How many calories in less sugar chocolate milk?
Is less sugar chocolate milk still an SSB?
What is the GI of chocolate milk?
Is chocolate milk (less sugar) suitable for weight loss?
Does less sugar chocolate milk have calcium?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record F01030002) with confidence 0.95 for all core macros and secondary nutrients. GI value estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous reduced-sugar chocolate milk entry. Saturated fat estimated at 1.0 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 01103 (Milk, chocolate, fluid, commercial, lowfat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans) are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry F01030002 for this specific less-sugar chocolate milk formulation as of 2026; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 01103 was consulted but the HPB entry is the primary source and does not include these fractions. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: same basis as monounsaturated_fat_g — not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; detailed fatty acid profile unavailable from primary source. trans_fat_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; trans fat data for this specific formulation is not available in the primary source as of 2026. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; no approved secondary source provides a confirmed value for this exact formulation. cholesterol_mg set to -1: cholesterol is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source for this formulation. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: vitamin C is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1: riboflavin is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1: niacin is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_b6_mg set to -1: vitamin B6 is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1: vitamin B12 is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: folate is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: vitamin D is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: vitamin E is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. phosphorus_mg set to -1: phosphorus is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. magnesium_mg set to -1: magnesium is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. zinc_mg set to -1: zinc is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. water_g set to -1: moisture content is not reported in HPB Foodo F01030002; not available from primary source. All -1 fields reflect genuine data gaps in the HPB Foodo primary source (F01030002) as of 2026; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 01103 was consulted as a secondary reference for saturated fat estimation only (adapted value 1.0 g/100 g, documented in nutrient_source). GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for reduced-sugar chocolate milk; the exact HPB F01030002 formulation has not been independently tested in a published GI study. The 'less sugar' designation does not eliminate SSB classification under Singapore HPB or AHA guidelines when sugar exceeds 5 g/100 g in a beverage. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 200 g glass: GL = (40 × 18.0) / 100 = 7.2.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Documented all -1 fields in the 'notes' section with a comprehensive explanation of why the data is unavailable from the primary source (HPB Foodo) and what alternative sources were consulted (USDA FoodData Central for saturated fat estimation). This addressed the G3 numeric hygiene hard gate failure from iteration 1.

Iteration 3: Expanded the 'notes' field to document each -1 field individually with an explicit statement of the missing-data basis and the source consulted, satisfying the G3 numeric hygiene requirement that each -1 value must have its missing-data basis documented. The validator confirmed all Q criteria passed in iteration 2; only G3 remained as a hard gate failure due to insufficient per-field documentation of -1 values.
